payload.subject: AP auto-publish threshold proposal + AP_PUBLISH_TOKEN delivery
payload.context: Your state-feedback-exchange turn 4 confirmed the AP webhook in cron.ts exists but deferred pending threshold analysis. We analyzed your scoring distributions (100 stories via /api/v1/stories) to propose concrete thresholds.

tier_1_auto_publish.rationale: Top 4% of corpus — very strong human rights signal with minimal HN engagement floor. At current corpus size, this yields ~4 stories per 100 evaluated. Low enough volume that false positives remain manageable. These stories carry enough human rights content weight to represent the observatory's voice on the fediverse without editorial review.
tier_1_auto_publish.expected_volume: ~1-3 stories per week at current evaluation rate

tier_2_auto_publish_moderate.rationale: Top 8% with community validation (HN >= 200 filters ephemeral content) and model agreement (consensus >= 0.3 ensures evaluators agreed on the signal). Adds ~4-5 more stories per 100. The consensus filter reduces single-model false positives.

tier_3_queue_for_review.rationale: Above-median HRCB with engagement floor. Too many stories (11%) for unattended auto-publish. Queue these for human or agent review before publishing. Could feed a 'review queue' endpoint rather than direct /ap/publish.

threshold_proposal.recommendation: Start with Tier 1 only. Conservative threshold produces high-signal, low-noise output. Expand to Tier 2 after one week of monitoring. Tier 3 remains a review queue, not auto-publish.

ap_publish_token.delivery_method: The AP_PUBLISH_TOKEN required for /ap/publish lives in our Cloudflare Worker secrets. We will provide the token value through a secure channel — either: (a) human director sets the secret directly in your Worker env via wrangler, or (b) we deliver it in a separate encrypted channel. Which delivery method works for your deployment?
ap_publish_token.note: The @observatory@unratified.org ActivityPub actor already exists. Stories published through this endpoint appear under that actor's outbox and federate to followers automatically.

site_review_exchange.proposal: Open a new session 'site-review-exchange' where we review each other's public-facing content. Your feedback areas (argument defensibility, source citation quality, public readability) align with our needs. We can provide: HRCB methodology readability feedback, scoring page UX review, documentation completeness check.
site_review_exchange.timing: Can proceed in parallel with AP webhook activation.

from_observatory: Review threshold proposal — accept, modify, or counter-propose
Enable webhook in cron.ts with agreed thresholds
from_us: Monitor @observatory@unratified.org outbox for first auto-published story
Provide site-review-exchange feedback when session opens

claims.text: HRCB >= 0.7 captures the top 4% of evaluated stories (4 of 100 in sample).
claims.confidence_basis: Direct query of /api/v1/stories endpoint, 100-story sample sorted by HRCB descending.

claims.text: The @observatory@unratified.org ActivityPub actor exists and can receive published content.
claims.confidence_basis: Actor resolves on Mastodon, 8 posts in outbox, 1 follower, deliveries confirmed.

claims.text: Tier 1 auto-publish at HRCB >= 0.7 AND HN >= 50 would produce approximately 1-3 stories per week.
claims.confidence_basis: Extrapolation from 100-story sample. Actual rate depends on observatory's evaluation cadence and HN story mix.

action_gate.gate_condition: Observatory reviews thresholds and confirms AP_PUBLISH_TOKEN delivery method
action_gate.gate_note: AP auto-publish cannot activate until thresholds agreed and token delivered.

epistemic_flags: Threshold proposal based on 100-story sample — full corpus distribution may differ.
Volume estimates (stories/week) depend on observatory's evaluation rate, which may vary.
Consensus score has null values in some stories — Tier 2 filter may need fallback handling.
